parameters.name: width

overloads.signature: dbamp[width: Int, //](db: SIMD[DType.float64, width]) -> SIMD[DType.float64, width]

overloads.name: ampdb

parameters.description: Size of the SIMD vector. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: width

overloads.signature: ampdb[width: Int, //](amp: SIMD[DType.float64, width]) -> SIMD[DType.float64, width]
overloads.summary: Converts amplitude values to decibels.

args.description: Minimum value to avoid log of zero.
args.name: amin

overloads.description: This mirrors librosa's power_to_db behavior for a single scalar: 10 * log10(max(amin, value) / zero_db_ref).
overloads.name: power_to_db
overloads.signature: power_to_db(value: Float64, zero_db_ref: Float64 = 1, amin: Float64 = 1.0E-10) -> Float64
overloads.summary: Convert a power value to decibels.

overloads.name: linlin

parameters.description: The data type of the SIMD vector. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: dtype

parameters.description: Size of the SIMD vector. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: num_chans

overloads.signature: linlin[dtype: DType, num_chans: Int, //](input: SIMD[dtype, num_chans], in_min: SIMD[dtype, num_chans] = 0, in_max: SIMD[dtype, num_chans] = 1, out_min: SIMD[dtype, num_chans] = 0, out_max: SIMD[dtype, num_chans] = 1) -> SIMD[dtype, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Maps samples from one range to another range linearly.

overloads.name: expexp
overloads.signature: expexp[num_chans: Int, //](input: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Exponential-to-exponential transform.

overloads.name: linexp
overloads.signature: linexp[num_chans: Int, //](input: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Maps samples from one linear range to another exponential range.

overloads.name: explin
overloads.signature: explin[num_chans: Int, //](input: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Exponential-to-linear transform (inverse of linexp).

args.description: The minimum of the input range.
args.name: in_min

args.description: The maximum of the input range.
args.name: in_max

args.description: The minimum of the output range (must be > 0).
args.name: out_min

args.description: The maximum of the output range (must be > 0).
args.name: out_max

args.description: The curve factor. Positive values create an exponential-like curve, negative values create a logarithmic-like curve, and zero results in a linear mapping.
args.name: curve

overloads.name: lincurve

parameters.description: Size of the SIMD vector - defaults to 1. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: num_chans

returns.doc: The curved mapped samples in the output range.

overloads.signature: lincurve[num_chans: Int, //](input: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], curve: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Maps samples from one linear range to another curved range.

args.description: Input value to transform (from curved space).
args.name: input

args.description:
Curve parameter (-10 to 10 typical range)
       curve = 0: linear
       curve > 0: undoes exponential curve
       curve < 0: undoes logarithmic curve.
args.name: curve

overloads.name: curvelin
overloads.signature: curvelin[num_chans: Int, //](input: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], in_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_min: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], out_max: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], curve: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans] = 0) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Curve-to-linear transform (inverse of lincurve).

overloads.description:
The wrapped sample within the range [min_val, max_val). 
This function uses modulus arithmetic so the output can never equal max_val.
Returns the sample if min_val >= max_val.
overloads.name: wrap

parameters.description: The data type of the SIMD vector. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: dtype

parameters.description: Size of the SIMD vector. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: num_chans

overloads.signature: wrap[dtype: DType, num_chans: Int, //](input: SIMD[dtype, num_chans], min_val: SIMD[dtype, num_chans], max_val: SIMD[dtype, num_chans]) -> SIMD[dtype, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Wraps a sample around a specified range.

args.description: The MIDI note number(s) to convert.
args.name: midi_note_number

args.description: The frequency of the reference MIDI note.
args.name: reference_frequency

overloads.description:
(cps = "cycles per second")

Conversion happens based on equating the `reference_midi_note` to the `reference_frequency`.
For standard tuning, leave the defaults of MIDI note 69 (A4) and 440.0 Hz.
overloads.name: midicps

parameters.description: Size of the SIMD vector. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: num_chans

overloads.signature: midicps[num_chans: Int, //](midi_note_number: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], reference_midi_note: Float64 = 69, reference_frequency: Float64 = 440) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Convert MIDI note numbers to frequencies in Hz.

args.description: The frequency in Hz to convert.
args.name: freq

args.description: The frequency of the reference MIDI note.
args.name: reference_frequency

overloads.description:
(cps = "cycles per second")

Conversion happens based on equating the `reference_midi_note` to the `reference_frequency`.
For standard tuning, leave the defaults of MIDI note 69 (A4) and 440.0 Hz.

parameters.description: Size of the SIMD vector. This parameter is inferred by the values passed to the function.
parameters.name: num_chans

overloads.signature: cpsmidi[num_chans: Int, //](freq: SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans], reference_midi_note: Float64 = 69, reference_frequency: Float64 = 440) -> SIMD[DType.float64, num_chans]
overloads.summary: Convert frequencies in Hz to MIDI note numbers.
